I apologize but I could not verify any information about "Kadurias convallariiflora". However, if you are referring to Convallaria majus (Lily of the valley), here's a paragraph about that plant:

Convallaria majus, commonly known as Lily of the valley, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the Asparagaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include reducing stress, anxiety, and insomnia due to its sedative effects, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Convallaria majus include flavonoids, glycosides, and cardiac glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lily of the valley is often used to make preparations such as tinctures, teas, and infusions to promote relaxation and relieve symptoms of heart conditions.
